reasoning: The question asks if there will be more 'Violence against civilians' in Saint Vincent and the Grenadines for three distinct 30-day windows compared to its 30-day average over the previous 360 days. Given the baseline of zero recorded incidents over an entire year, the likelihood of an event abruptly occurring within any of the specific 1-month windows in the near future is extremely low. Even accounting for the possibility that 2025 is an election year (based on the 2020 election cycle) which might introduce some tension, large-scale organized violence against civilians remains highly uncharacteristic. Following instructions to bound probabilities at 0.05 and increase uncertainty for distant forecasting horizons, the probability estimates are pinned at the minimum allowable boundary, with slight upward adjustments for later resolution dates to reflect horizon uncertainty.
